what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG outline icon showing two horizontal arrows pointing in opposite directions to represent swap or exchange.
observations: Uses an SVG root with viewBox 0 0 24 24 and fixed width and height of 24.
Uses fill="none" and stroke="currentColor" so the icon inherits text color.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Contains two path elements: a top arrow pointing right and a bottom arrow pointing left.
standouts: Bidirectional swap metaphor is encoded with two opposing horizontal arrows.
Icon styling is controlled through currentColor and stroke-only paths.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions: 24px canvas, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded stroke endings.
Uses compact inline path data without separate groups, titles, or metadata.
concerns: No title, role, or aria attributes are present; accessibility semantics must come from the consumer if needed.
notes: A small themeable swap/exchange SVG icon built from two opposing stroked arrows.
questions: Is this icon intended to be purely decorative, or should consumers provide an accessible label when used?
Should fixed width and height remain on the asset, or should sizing be controlled entirely by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: The file name and path indicate a swap icon asset, and the SVG depicts opposing arrows consistent with that purpose.
intention_signals: It appears intended as a reusable design-system icon for swap, exchange, or direction reversal actions.
contribution: Provides a compact, color-inheriting bidirectional swap glyph that a fused system would keep as an exchange/action icon asset.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px stroke-only SVG swap icon made of two opposing horizontal arrows using currentColor styling.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing swap or exchange actions in a reusable icon set.
